channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Catherine Herridge wants Trump 'sit-down' on bipartisan press bill
item.description: “This case involves the First Amendment and the right of reporters in the future,” Catherine Herridge said.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Alabama carries out nation’s third nitrogen gas execution on a man for a hitchhiker's killing
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted in the 1994 killing of a hitchhiker cursed at the prison warden and made obscene gestures with his hands shortly before he was put to death Thursday evening in the nation’s third execution using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pathologist disputes finding that Marine veteran's chokehold caused subway rider's death
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For roughly six minutes, Jordan Neely was pinned to a subway floor in a chokehold that ended with him lying still. But that's not what killed him, a forensic pathologist testified Thursday in defense of the military-trained commuter charged with killing Neely. Dr.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Prolific Mexican cartel leader allegedly faked his own death to live 'life of luxury' in California
item.description: A Mexican cartel leader, who peddled thousands of kilograms of illegal drugs to the U.S., was killed by a business associate for "lying" — or at least that's what the public was told.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KREX 5

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KREX 5

item.title: Democrat Bob Casey concedes to Republican David McCormick in Pennsylvania Senate contest
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Democratic Sen. Bob Casey of Pennsylvania conceded his reelection bid to Republican David McCormick on Thursday, as a statewide recount showed no signs of closing the gap and his campaign suffered blows in court in its effort to get favorable ballots counted.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Netanyahu ‘in very bad company’ with ICC indictment: William Cohen
item.description: The ICC issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and a former defense minister on war crimes allegations.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Dakota Republican introduces bill to eliminate Department of Education
item.description: The proposal would get rid of the federal agency and move all critical programs to other departments.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senators express relief over Gaetz’s decision to withdraw from AG consideration
item.description: Sen. Roger Wicker (R-Miss.), the incoming chair of the Senate Armed Services Committee, welcomed Gaetz’s decision to withdraw as “a positive development.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Police warn of sophisticated holiday scam artists targeting consumers
item.description: He said any time shoppers use their credit cards or personal information, they should go in with their eyes open.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth, Trump's pick for defense secretary
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA, BRIAN SLODYSKO and JULIET LINDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about a storm bringing high winds, heavy rain, snow to California and Pacific Northwest
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — One of the strongest storms on the West Coast in decades knocked out power for thousands of people, unleashed strong winds that toppled trees and left two dead in Washington before making its way through Oregon to Northern California where on Thursday it dropped heavy snow and r
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: North Carolina official overseeing hurricane rebuilding efforts is no longer in role
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The top official for North Carolina's efforts to rebuild communities after natural disasters is no longer working in the position just a few days after a scathing rebuke of her work from GOP state lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thanksgiving meal deals: Here's what grocery stores are offering
item.description: Major grocers like Walmart and Target are offering "inflation-free" Thanksgiving meal deals. Here's what you can buy.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Historically bad year for dolphin strandings on Cape Cod has scientists looking for answers
item.description: Scientists say an unprecedentedly bad year for beached dolphins on Cape Cod might have to do with warming waters changing the availability of the animals’ food.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump has promised again to release the last JFK files. But experts say don’t expect big revelations
item.description: The nation is set to mark 61 years since President John F. Kennedy was assassinated as his motorcade passed through downtown Dallas on Nov. 22, 1963.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Known St. Louis-based Neo-Nazi group takes credit for Ohio disturbance
item.description: We get close to the line of whether it’s freedom of speech or the attempt to intimidate,” St. Louis City NAACP President Adolphus Pruitt said.
item.dc:creator: Max Diekneite, NewsNation

item.title: What happened to JonBenét Ramsey? New documentary set to premiere this month
item.description: The death of JonBenét Ramsey remains unsolved and has been the subject of a number of documentaries and books.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, NewsNation

item.title: Attorneys want the US Supreme Court to say Mississippi's felony voting ban is cruel and unusual
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — The U.S. Supreme Court should overturn Mississippi's Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft, attorneys say in new court papers.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meghan Markle's bad week as Prince Harry outshines his wife
item.description: Across the pond in America, former British royal, Prince Harry, is making inroads to make himself likable again… and it’s working.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: OpenTable names 2024's 'Top 100 Restaurants' in America
item.description: OpenTable's analysts based their findings on more than 14 million diner reviews, along with diner ratings, reservation demand, and percentage of five-star reviews, among other factors.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Whooping cough cases have quintupled. These states have it worst
item.description: Pertussis – a disease commonly referred to as whooping cough – is spreading quickly, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention warns. The U.S. has seen five times more cases in 2024 than we did at this time last year.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illinois Supreme Court reverses actor Jussie Smollett's conviction
item.description: The Illinois Supreme Court has reversed the conviction of actor Jussie Smollett, who was found guilty of falsely reporting a 2019 hate crime, after he argued that the State's second prosecution was a due process violation.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: St. Louis was once known as Mound City for its many Native American mounds. Just one remains
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — What is now St. Louis was once home to more than 100 mounds constructed by Native Americans — so many that St. Louis was once known as “Mound City.” Settlers tore most of them down, and just one remains.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ukraine says Russia launched an intercontinental missile in an attack for the first time in the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a missile used “matches the speed and altitude” of an ICBM. The Kremlin refused to comment on the strike.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, ABC 4

item.title: Sing-along screenings of 'Wicked' coming soon as AMC issues strict warning to fans
item.description: Fans eager to belt out hits like "Defying Gravity" and "Popular" in theaters during the highly anticipated "Wicked" movie musical will need to hold off — for now.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, ABC 4

item.title: Attack of the trees: Aging giants wreak havoc in Missouri neighborhood
item.description: "We’re pretty concerned. We’re thinking, ‘Should we move before the trees destroy our home?’” said one resident.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC tourist stabbed in swanky area after 3 killed in random attack as DA slams Trump
item.description: A 55-year-old tourist was slashed in his face on the Upper West Side in New York City in another unprovoked street stabbing, police confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, KRON4

item.title: Ex-student pleads guilty to fatally shooting 3 University of Virginia football players in 2022
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — A former University of Virginia student pleaded guilty Wednesday to fatally shooting three football players and wounding two other students on the campus in 2022. Christopher Darnell Jones Jr.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and DENISE LAVOIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Susan Smith denied parole 30 years after drowning sons by rolling car into South Carolina lake
item.description: Susan Smith made national headlines in 1994 after she rolled her car into a lake with both of her children strapped in their seats.
item.dc:creator: Dustin George, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Democrats ask FBI for files on Gaetz sex trafficking probe
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee have sent a letter to FBI Director Christopher Wray asking for “the complete evidentiary file” from the bureau's investigation into allegations of sex trafficking of minors against former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Former 'American Idol' contestant pleads guilty in deadly DUI crash
item.description: Former "American Idol" contestant Caleb Kennedy has pleaded guilty to driving under the influence during a deadly 2022 crash in South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Robert Cox, Fox 5

item.title: Malcom X estate alleges DOJ, FBI, CIA, NYPD played role in events leading to assassination
item.description: The estate of Malcolm X is suing the federal government, accusing it of playing a role in the 1965 assassination of the civil rights leader.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WGN 9
